tokens.spacing_notes: Two competing accent blues in active use (#1b6fc4 solid buttons vs #1f7fd0 link/dropdown text). Page-header band color is inconsistent: navy (#0d3a63) on most screens but WHITE on Blocklists. Primary action buttons are mostly solid blue rounded-4, but Deploy uses a ghost/outline button on navy and Devices uses text-only 'Export CSV' — divergent button roles. Table headers split between gray uppercase (#5b6b7b on Devices/Events/File Analysis) and BLUE uppercase (#2f6fb0 on Blocklists/Exclusions). Status indicators inconsistent: amber severity PILL on Events vs plain green TEXT 'Protected'/'Up-to-date' elsewhere vs gray scope chips on Exclusions. Help/Documentation is a centered marketing page in a different visual register from the dense app grids. Card corners ~4px, generous card gutters (~16-24px); KPI tiles use thin 1px gray borders, no shadows.

tokens.font_family_guess: Sans-serif; UI body looks like Open Sans / Segoe-style; VIPRE logo wordmark is a custom geometric italic. (No Rubik — this is the legacy product, not the Vipre Design System POC.)
tokens.spacing_notes: Tables range from comfortable (Applications) to ultra-dense (Quarantine) with inconsistent row heights. Two distinct tab idioms coexist: horizontal segmented/underline tabs vs vertical chevron-notch card rails (Policies, System). Toolbar treatment inconsistent — Sites uses a saturated blue bar with white pill filters while Applications/Vulnerabilities use plain blue-text caret dropdowns on white. Login page is a clearly different design era (peach primary button, EDR logo lockup, dotted-halftone photo bg) vs the in-app Site Manager navy theme. Loading splash is a photographic gradient with no chrome.

tokens.font_family_guess: Humanist/neo-grotesque sans-serif (looks like a system or Open Sans / Source Sans style stack); not the Rubik used in the Vipre DS POC
tokens.spacing_notes: Dense enterprise admin spacing: ~16-24px card padding, tight table rows (~8-10px), generous whitespace on onboarding/empty screens. Cards use soft 1px borders + light shadows on a #f4f6f8 body. Two distinct primary blues in use (#2f6fdb vivid vs #5b86c4 muted) signalling button inconsistency. Page-title typography is disproportionately large relative to body/table text.

tokens.font_family_guess: system/Helvetica-like sans-serif (no obvious custom brand font; bold geometric for nav 'VIPRE SYMPHONY' and 'EMAIL SECURITY' lockup)
tokens.spacing_notes: Consistent left nav rail ~222px. Content uses generous panel padding with white cards on a light grey (#e9ebee) canvas. Tables are flat with horizontal rules only (no vertical borders, no zebra striping). Two distinct icon weights coexist: thin white line icons in the nav rail vs heavy black filled glyphs on the Reports launcher cards. Three header strips stack at top: dark grey tenant breadcrumb bar, navy page-title band, then content. Empty/no-data states are text-only (no illustrations). Some captures rendered at very small zoom (Message Logs/Threat Explorer populated) and two empty captures are clipped on the right, indicating mid-load snapshots.

tokens.font_family_guess: Rubik (or similar geometric sans); nav/headings use a heavier condensed weight, body is regular sans-serif
tokens.spacing_notes: Cards use ~24-32px internal padding on wide screens. Nav item rows ~40-44px tall. All-caps micro-labels (12px) with ~8px gap above values are a recurring form/data pattern. Two header heights observed (~110px tall on wide renders, ~58px on narrow renders) and two nav widths (~218px expanded, ~110px narrow), indicating the shared chrome scales/responds inconsistently across captures. Promo/marketing surfaces (overview upsell, EDR, SAML) use larger type and rounded pill buttons that diverge from the squared 6px primary buttons in the admin tables.

tokens.font_family_guess: Humanist/geometric sans-serif (looks like Lato or Open Sans / system UI). Page titles appear in a slightly different weighted sans than body. Not Rubik (the Vipre DS POC font).
tokens.spacing_notes: 8px-based card padding (~16-20px internal). KPI tiles ~12-16px padding. Dense explorer tables use ~6-8px row padding; light list tables use ~14-16px row height. Inconsistent corner radii: list-table search inputs are pill (~20px) while explorer/dark search inputs are 6px rectangles.

screens.layout_notes: Left nav-sidebar dark navy (~210px). Secondary vertical scope-tab rail (light blue) between sidebar and content with 'Billed Sites'/'Trial Sites' chevron tabs. Dark navy page-header band with white 'Usage' title. Content is white, low-density. Custom Bootstrap-ish enterprise look; product-tile chips (EDR+MDR / EDR / ESC) with gradient/solid fills sit above a line+bar chart.

Left dark navy nav-sidebar. Vertical scope-tab rail with single 'Windows' tab. Page-header band is WHITE here (not navy) with large black 'Blocklists' title and a blue solid 'Add Blocklist' button top-right. Two empty tables with blue uppercase column headers and 'No blocklist found.' empty states. Lower density.

Dark navy nav-sidebar + dark navy page-header band ('Dashboard'). High-density KPI row of bordered tiles across top, then a wide multi-series line/area trend chart, and a bottom row of donut charts + a teal/green 'RESEARCH' promo card. Material-ish/custom hybrid; rounded-card panels.

Dark navy nav-sidebar + navy page-header band with white 'Deploy' title and a SEMI-TRANSPARENT outline 'Approve Latest Version' button (different from the solid blue Add buttons elsewhere). Vertical OS scope-tab rail (Windows/Mac/Linux) with platform icons. Light-blue 'LATEST VERSION' banner card. Filter pill row (ALL SITES/Outdated/Current Version) + search input. Single table, low density.

Dark navy nav-sidebar + navy page-header band ('Devices') with white 'Export CSV' link/button and an 'Actions' button top-right plus a search icon. SECOND faceted-filter sidebar (white, checkbox tree: Platform/Type/Status/Policy/Agent) sits between main nav and the data table — distinct from other screens. Very high-density table. Bootstrap-ish enterprise grid.

Dark navy nav-sidebar + navy page-header band ('EDR Dashboard'). Body is a grid of bordered white card panels: two top KPI summary cards (Active/Resolved Incidents), then 6-card grid mixing 'Top 10' ranked lists with line charts. Light, airy density vs the main Dashboard. Card style is flat with thin gray borders, gray uppercase card titles.

Dark navy nav-sidebar + navy page-header band ('Events') with search icon and a 'TIME:' range control top-right. Left faceted-filter sidebar (Site/Platform/Device/Event Type/Severity/MITRE ATT&CK checkboxes). Dense data table with a MEDIUM severity badge and blue link cells. Bootstrap-ish enterprise grid.

Dark navy nav-sidebar + navy page-header band ('Exclusions') with blue solid 'Add Exclusion List' button top-right. Vertical OS scope-tab rail (Windows/Mac/Linux/Android/Chromebook) with platform icons. Sectioned tables; rows show blue link names plus small scope badges ('Global'/'Policy') with a shield/check icon. Medium density.

Dark navy nav-sidebar + navy page-header band ('File Analysis') with blue solid 'Upload File' button (cloud icon) top-right. No scope rail. Inline DATE range control ('From ... to now') with blue link-style values, and a FILTER row of dropdown-menu triggers (Submission Status / Risk / User / Site) plus a gear settings icon. Empty table 'No analyzed files found.' Rendered at larger scale; clean Bootstrap-ish.

Dark navy nav-sidebar + navy page-header band ('Documentation'). Vertical scope-tab rail (Documentation/Diagnostics/Support). Content is a centered marketing-style documentation landing page: sectioned headings ('Online Documentation','First Steps','Get Visibility','More Customization and Power') with icon+title+paragraph link cards in 2-4 column grids. Very different visual register (content/marketing page vs app screens). Footer with blue legal trademark links.
Left dark-navy nav sidebar + dark-navy page header bar with white title; full-width data table below on white body. Light-blue (#eaf2fb) hover row striping. Consistent VIPRE Site Manager shell.

Same shell. Segmented tab control (Ungrouped / Grouped by ID / Grouped by Device) under header. A small inline scatter/dot trend chart + two KPI count tiles at top, then a dense data table. Multiple inline filter dropdowns above table. Export CSV button top-right.

Vertical OS tab rail (Windows/Mac/Linux/Android) with arrow-tab/chevron pointer styling on light-blue panel — a DIFFERENT tab idiom than the horizontal segmented tabs elsewhere. Three 'Policy Assignment' data-cards in a row (Laptops/Workstations/Servers). Radio group + checkbox filter row. Empty-state table ('No policies found.'). 'Add Policy' primary button top-right.

Two-pane: left faceted-filter sidebar (Platform / Severity / Category / Source checkboxes) on white, right very dense data table with colored severity text. No page header bar visible (table starts at top). Numbered pagination at bottom. Denser, older-looking table than the Applications screen.

Marketing/explainer style page — distinct from data-table screens. URL input with circular send button, illustrated diagram, 'How does it work?' / 'How does it integrate?' sections with screenshot thumbnails framed in orange annotation boxes. Feels like a different design era (content/onboarding page).
Card grid (6-up) of report tiles, each an outlined light-bordered card with a centered dark-navy icon and 2-line title. Clean, modern card layout — distinct from dense tables.

Horizontal underline tabs (Sites / Site Managers). Bright blue (#1b75bb) filter/toolbar bar with pill toggle filters (All Sites / Expired / Needing Attention / Billed) and rounded search input. Table with seats progress bar and a small area sparkline in THREATS column. Note: this state rendered at a wider/zoomed scale than other screens.

Same Sites page with a row expanded into a 3-column detail panel (Contract / Operational / Security) containing mini KPI stats, a stacked device-type bar, sparkline area trend charts, and a top-threats mini list. Mixed card/inline-stat composition.

Full-screen branded loading splash: blue building-photo gradient background with centered white 'Loading' text. Top breadcrumb tab bar present. DIFFERENT visual era — photographic gradient, no app chrome.

DIFFERENT design era / standalone auth page. Dark blue dotted-halftone photographic background, centered white card split into 'New Features' (left, icon list) and 'Sign in' (right, email/password). Uses a VIPRE EDR logo (not Site Manager) and a PEACH/SALMON (#f0a878) primary Sign In button — an accent color not seen anywhere else in the product.

Vertical chevron/arrow tab rail (Notifications / Webhooks / Keys / Customer Support) with stacked icon+label tabs — same arrow-tab idiom as Policies. Empty-state table ('No notifications found.'). 'Add Notification' primary button top-right. Rendered at wider scale like the Sites screens.

Dashboard layout: top row of stat/KPI summary tiles, a large bar+scatter timeline 'Threat Trend' chart center, two threat lists flanking, plus bottom 'Severity Breakdown' horizontal bar chart, a sites-with-most-threats bar, and an 'Actions Taken' donut chart with legend. Multiple chart types on one canvas.

Dark navy fixed left sidebar (~220px) + light gray body. Dark navy top bar carries a 'New Rule' primary blue button. Page title 'Allow List' with a small shield glyph rendered large/bold at top of content. White search bar spanning full width with a blue magnifier search button on the right. Below: a white table card with a disabled gray 'Delete' bulk-action button top-right, header row in light gray, and a 'No data' / 'Found 0 matches' empty state. House style is a dense enterprise admin look distinct from a modern token-based DS; mixes very large page title typography with small table type.

Same dark sidebar shell. Content is a single full-width white table card titled 'Audit events' with a 'TIME' date-range chip top-right and an 'Add column +' control. Dense, many-row data table (Timestamp/User/Action/Target/Error columns). Bottom shows 'Found 320 matches' and numbered pagination (1 2 3 4 5 ... ) with prev/next arrows. Classic enterprise data-grid era.

Settings form layout: white card on light gray body holding stacked sections ('Sandbox', 'Microsoft 365 Allow List') each with a bold heading, helper text, a bulleted list of file extensions, and an iOS-style pill toggle switch ('Enable ...'). Toggles appear off (gray). Calm form-centric layout.

Onboarding/selector screen. Centered prompt 'Select a source below to configure and add to IES.' with three large equal white selection cards (Microsoft 365 / IMAP / Google Workspace), each with a brand glyph and bold label, subtle rounded corners and shadow. Distinct centered marketing-style layout vs the data-grid screens.

Populated state of Email Configuration. Top bar has three blue primary buttons (Add IMAP / Add M365 / Add Google Workspace) each with an icon. Body shows two side-by-side white data-cards per connected source, each with brand glyph + name, green 'CONNECTED' status badge, a refresh icon and kebab menu, key/value meta rows (Type/Mode/Source ID), two inline metric numbers with tiny red down-arrows ('Emails in the last week / last 24 hours'), and a small sparkline/area mini-chart with Jun 11-17 axis. Mixed card+metric+chart composition.

Empty-state dashboard. Large white card filling body with a faint dotted/graph-paper background, a centered line-art envelope+gears illustration, heading 'Start your account configuration', a paragraph of muted body copy, and a single blue 'Get started!' primary button. Friendly onboarding empty state.

Dense analytics dashboard. Top row of KPI tiles separated by vertical dividers (Emails 27, Internal/External 3, Attachments 3, Users 163, Data 17, Clean 11, plus more) with small colored icons. 'TIME' date chip top-right. 'Emails vs Threats' wide combo chart (bars + area lines) with a legend (Emails/Phishing/Suspicious/Malware). Row of small white list-cards (Top Attackers, Top Threat Tactics, Top Targets, Top Targeted Groups) with mini tables. Second row: a treemap ('Top Threat Types' Malware/Spam/Unrated), horizontal bar 'Threat Severity' and 'Threat Vectors' meters, and a donut/bubble 'Top Engines by Detections'. Full-width 'Attack Origins' world map with plotted points and + zoom control. Heaviest, most chart-rich screen; busy enterprise BI aesthetic.

Settings screen with a horizontal tab strip (URL Filter Configuration / Allowed IPs/Domains / Denied IPs/Domains / Templates). Body is a long form: master 'Enable Link Isolation' toggle, sub-sections (Click-time Protection, URL Rewriting) with radio groups (Block / Click Through), a numbered 'EMAIL TIMEOUT (SECONDS)' input, and multiple toggles. A blue 'Save Changes' button bottom-right. Tabbed form pattern not seen on other screens.

Left dark navy nav rail + light content area. This capture is in a 'No data' empty state: line chart shows flat axes with 'No data' centered, filter rail items all read 'No data', table reads 'Found 0 matches'. Screenshot is partially rendered/clipped on the right (white gap, a stray pill-shaped element peeking) suggesting a mid-load capture. Consistent single design era with the other captures.

Same shell as the empty capture but fully populated: stacked/colored area chart with red+orange peaks, dense data table of email messages with verdict/severity badges, left FILTER rail now shows checkbox facet groups (Status, Verdict, Severity, Engines, Threat Type, etc.) with counts, bottom pagination. Captured at a smaller zoom so type is tiny. Same design library as reports/users pages.

Simplest page: three large white report-launcher cards (Link Click Analysis, Top Targeted Users, Top Threat Senders) each with a centered black glyph icon and bold label, on a light grey canvas. Top breadcrumb chip bar 'DEV - Monthly Reseller - DO NOT DELETE > Zack IES Safesend Combo'. No filter rail, no table. Card icon style (heavy black filled glyphs) differs from the thin nav-rail icons.

Visually identical to the other Reports capture; only the top breadcrumb tenant chip differs ('qa_admin13' vs 'Zack IES Safesend Combo'). Same three white launcher cards, same grey canvas. Useful as a consistency baseline - this page renders identically across the two captures.

Same shell as Message Logs but adds a row of four KPI/summary tiles (Attack Severity, Attack Vector, Detection Engines, Engine Verdict Dist...) above the table. This capture is empty: line chart 'No data', tiles 'No data', table 'Found 0 matches'. Captured at small zoom and clipped on the right (white gap). Same design era.

Fully populated Threat Explorer: stacked area chart up top, then four summary tiles where Attack Severity/Attack Vector/Detection Engines/Engine Verdict render as donut/pie charts, then a dense email table with many columns (Verdict, Severity, Sender IP, Detection Engine, Threat Types, Attack Vectors, Tactics, Status) and badges. Left checkbox filter rail populated. Same library as Message Logs.

Users directory table: columns Name, Email, Groups, Department, Source, Location. No left filter rail (just the standard nav rail) and no chart row - simpler than Message Logs/Threat Explorer. Avatars are colored circular initials. Blue 'Export' button top-right. Bottom pagination 'Found 22 matches'. 'Groups' cells use blue '+1 other' expander links. Same shell/design era.

Same Users table layout, different tenant data ('Found 163 matches', groups like MSFT / Journaling Test). Source column mixes 'Microsoft 365' and 'IMAP'. Same Export button and pagination. Consistent with the other Users capture.

Full-page light gray (#e9eaeb) canvas with two stacked white rounded data-cards (Account Details, Products). Left nav is in EXPANDED state (~218px) with light separator lines under IES/SafeSend sub-groups. Header is the tall navy variant (~110px). Products section uses nested outlined sub-cards with all-caps gray metric labels. Consistent Symphony chrome era.

Rendered at a NARROWER/compact viewport (~778px wide) so left nav is collapsed-to-narrow (~110px, icon+label small), header is short (~58px). Single white card holds a dense table with per-row kebab menus. Identical content to the wider Admins capture but at different zoom/density — useful for detecting responsive divergence.

Same Admins screen and same narrow viewport as the (1) duplicate — short header (~58px), narrow nav (~110px). Table of full name + email with kebab row actions, 'Found 23 matches' footer count. Blue 'Add Admin' button top-right in header.

Narrow viewport (~778px). Marketing splash on pale blue (#eaf1f9) background — large display heading, orange 'COMING SOON!' eyebrow, product screenshot collage on the right, solid blue 'Learn More' CTA. Different visual era from the data screens: promotional layout, no card chrome, oversized typography.

Rendered very small/zoomed-out (~390px). Light-blue framed dashboard panel containing: a centered SafeSend avatar chip, a Sankey/flow chart ('Email Action Journey') with a big '333 Total Emails' KPI, a combo bar+line chart ('Emails vs Actions') with a legend, two list panels ('Top Modifications', 'Top Warned Users'), and a bottom 'Complete Your Security Suite' upsell card with a purple 'Configure' button. Visually the most chart-heavy / distinct design era — pastel gradient panel, multi-color charts, rounded inner cards.

Narrow viewport (~778px). Nav shows a DIFFERENT product set (SafeSend + EDR expanded, IES locked) and a DIFFERENT breadcrumb tenant (Gus Endpoint Distributor) — this account has no dashboard data, so Overview renders only the 'Complete Your Security Suite' empty/upsell state. Two side-by-side product promo cards: SafeSend (pale pink dotted, purple 'Configure') and Integrated Email Security (dark navy gradient, white 'Start a Free Trial'). The two cards are styled inconsistently with each other.

Wide viewport, expanded nav (~218px), tall navy header. Single white card form: read-only Email/Role text, two text inputs (First/Last Name, gray-filled), one native-style select (Time Zone), a disabled gray 'Save' button, and two muted slate secondary buttons (Change Two-Factor Authentication, Change Password) separated by hairline dividers. Standard Symphony form era.

Wide viewport, expanded nav, tall navy header with blue 'Add Role' CTA. Single white card with a 4-column access-matrix table (Role Name / Symphony / IES / SafeSend). Pill 'Default' badges next to system roles; trash-icon row action only on custom rows. 'Found 5 matches' footer. Tinted gray-blue header row, no row striping.

Narrow viewport (~778px). Single white card with explanatory paragraph, a slate 'Configure SAML' button with gear icon, and a large decorative blue line-art security illustration on the right. Empty/intro state styling.

Dark navy left sidebar + dark navy topbar, but white/light content canvas. Content area uses light cards on near-white background. KPI row of bordered outline tiles, a combined bar+axis chart, three 'Top ___' list cards, two treemap-style distribution panels. This is the 'light content' design era of the app.

Light content canvas. Single white card containing a draggable priority list with drag-handle dots, avatar+name rows, small count pills, right-aligned policy link, and an X remove icon per row. Solid filled 'Add Assignment' button in topbar (different button style than the outline KPI era).

FULLY DARK theme content area (dark navy table on dark background) — distinctly different design era/skin from the light Dashboard/Policies pages. Left faceted filter rail, dense data-heavy log table with colored status text, many inline icons, badge counts, bottom pagination with rows-per-page select. Looks like a separate, denser 'explorer' library.

Near-identical duplicate of the other Policies capture. Light content canvas, borderless table with link-styled policy names, info icons, a 'Default' badge, Clone text-link action + X icon, numbered pill pagination.

Light content canvas. Borderless/ruled table, blue link policy names with leading info (i) icon, light-blue 'Default' badge, rounded search input top-right, 'Clone' text link + X icon row actions, numbered pill pagination with chevron arrows. Solid 'Add Policy' button in topbar.

FULLY DARK theme content area like Events Explorer — different era from light pages. Top filter date/select row, three chart cards (two donut/pie, one line trend), then a dense dark table with pill status badges and attachment icons, numbered pagination. Multi-color pie palette stands out vs. rest of app.

Light content canvas, matches Policies styling. Sortable table (caret on Name column), blue link names, numeric columns, date column, 'Clone' link + X row actions, numbered pill pagination. Solid 'Add Ruleset' button in topbar.

Light content canvas but with an 'explorer' flavor: Rules/Keywords tab toggle, search with solid dark search-button, a row of bordered KPI tiles with circular icon chips, then a table with sortable column headers + caret affordances, inline warning triangle icons, a gear/column-config icon, and a 'Rows 25' select + arrow pagination. Mix of the light-card era and the explorer-table era.

Light content canvas, form/marketing-document style. Green success banner, status key-value block, a logo card with 'Edit logo' outline button, and two 'manifest configuration' option blocks each with a blue solid 'Download' button. Long-form copy, different rhythm than data tables.

FULLY DARK theme content area — another dark/explorer-skinned page. Very dense three-column editable string table (key / default text / English text) with per-row edit pencil icons, alternating subtle row shading, green 'Publish/Save' style button in topbar. High-density admin grid.

DARK theme content area like Events Explorer. Row of KPI tiles up top (incl. a 'Default Policy' info tile), then dense user table with colored circular avatar initials, numeric counts, 'Default Policy' link cells, relative + absolute timestamp 'Last Activity', gear column-config icon, rows-per-page select pagination.
